# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std/http/server.ts";
import { openKv } from "https://deno.land/x/deno_kv/mod.ts";

interface User {
  id: string;
  [key: string]: any;
}

async function handleRequest(request: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const url = new URL(request.url);
  const kv = await openKv();
  const userId = url.searchParams.get('userId');
  const filterParam = url.searchParams.get('filterParam');

  if (!userId || !filterParam) {
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: "Missing userId or filterParam" }), { status: 400 });
  }

  try {
    const key = ['users', userId, filterParam];
    const result = await kv.get(key);

    if (result) {
      return new Response(JSON.stringify(result), { status: 200 });
    } else {
      return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: "No data found" }), { status: 404 });
    }
  } catch (error) {
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: "Failed to retrieve data", details: error.message }), { status: 500 });
  } finally {
    kv.close();
  }
}

serve(handleRequest);
